debug_info.goal:
R : Type ?u.16996
inst✝ : CommRing R
I J : Ideal R
⊢ ∀ {a b : R}, a ∈ ↑I ∩ ↑J → b ∈ ↑I ∩ ↑J → a + b ∈ ↑I ∩ ↑J

intro a b ha hb
    have h₁ : a + b ∈ I := by
      have h₂ : a ∈ I := ha.1
      have h₃ : b ∈ I := hb.1
      exact Ideal.add_mem I h₂ h₃
    have h₂ : a + b ∈ J := by
      have h₃ : a ∈ J := ha.2
      have h₄ : b ∈ J := hb.2
      exact Ideal.add_mem J h₃ h₄
    exact ⟨h₁, h₂⟩
  zero_mem' := by
    have h₁ : (0 : R) ∈ I := Ideal.zero_mem I
    have h₂ : (0 : R) ∈ J := Ideal.zero_mem J
    exact ⟨h₁, h₂⟩
  mul_mem' := by
    intro a b ha
    have h₁ : a ∈ I := ha.1
    have h₂ : a ∈ J := ha.2
    have h₃ : a * b ∈ I := Ideal.mul_mem_right I h₁ b
    have h₄ : a * b ∈ J := Ideal.mul_mem_right J h₂ b
    exact ⟨h₃, h₄⟩
  mul_mem_left_right' := by
    intro a b ha
    have h₁ : a ∈ I := ha.1
    have h₂ : a ∈ J := ha.2
    have h₃ : b * a ∈ I := Ideal.mul_mem_left I b h₁
    have h₄ : b * a ∈ J := Ideal.mul_mem_left J b h₂
    exact ⟨h₃, h₄⟩
  smul_mem' := by
    intro a r ha
    have h₁ : a ∈ I := ha.1
    have h₂ : a ∈ J := ha.2
    have h₃ : r • a ∈ I := Ideal.smul_mem I r h₁
    have h₄ : r • a ∈ J := Ideal.smul_mem J r h₂
    exact ⟨h₃, h₄⟩
  one_mem' := by
    have h₁ : (1 : R) ∈ I := Ideal.one_mem I
    have h₂ : (1 : R) ∈ J := Ideal.one_mem J
    exact ⟨h₁, h₂⟩

    -- Since a and b are in both I and J, their sum is in both I and J.
    have haI : a ∈ I := by exact ha.1
    have haJ : a ∈ J := by exact ha.2
    have hbI : b ∈ I := by exact hb.1
    have hbJ : b ∈ J := by exact hb.2

    have haJ : a ∈ J := ha.2

intro a b ha hb
    constructor <;>
    (try simp_all [Set.mem_inter_iff]) <;>
    (try exact Ideal.add_mem (I := I) (by tauto) (by tauto)) <;>
    (try exact Ideal.add_mem (I := J) (by tauto) (by tauto))
  zero_mem' := by
    have h : (0 : R) ∈ (I : Set R) := Ideal.zero_mem I
    have h' : (0 : R) ∈ (J : Set R) := Ideal.zero_mem J
    exact ⟨h, h'⟩
  mul_mem' := by
    intro a b ha
    have h₁ : a * b ∈ (I : Set R) := Ideal.mul_mem_left (I := I) ha.1 b
    have h₂ : a * b ∈ (J : Set R) := Ideal.mul_mem_left (I := J) ha.2 b
    exact ⟨h₁, h₂⟩

intro a b ha hb
    -- Since I and J are ideals, they are closed under addition.
    -- Therefore, a + b is in both I and J, hence in their intersection.
    exact ⟨I.add_mem ha.1 hb.1, J.add_mem ha.2 hb.2⟩

intro a b ha hb
    -- Since a is in the intersection of I and J, it is in both I and J.
    -- Similarly for b.
    -- Therefore, a + b is in both I and J.
    have ha₁ : a ∈ I := by simpa using ha.1
    have ha₂ : a ∈ J := by simpa using ha.2
    have hb₁ : b ∈ I := by simpa using hb.1
    have hb₂ : b ∈ J := by simpa using hb.2
    -- Since I and J are ideals, they are closed under addition.
    -- Thus, a + b is in both I and J.
    have h₁ : a + b ∈ I := I.add_mem ha₁ hb₁
    have h₂ : a + b ∈ J := J.add_mem ha₂ hb₂
    -- Therefore, a + b is in the intersection of I and J.
    exact ⟨h₁, h₂⟩

intro a b ⟨ha, hb⟩ ⟨ha', hb'⟩
    exact ⟨I.add_mem ha ha', J.add_mem hb hb'⟩

intro a b ha hb
    refine' ⟨_, _⟩
    · -- Prove that a + b ∈ I

    · -- Prove that a + b ∈ J

intro a b ha hb
    -- Since I and J are ideals, they are closed under addition.
    -- So a + b ∈ I and a + b ∈ J.
    -- Therefore, a + b ∈ I ∩ J.
    have h₁ : a + b ∈ I := by
      -- a + b ∈ I because I is an ideal and closed under addition.
      have h₁ : a ∈ I := ha.1
      have h₂ : b ∈ I := hb.1
      exact Ideal.add_mem I h₁ h₂
    have h₂ : a + b ∈ J := by
      -- a + b ∈ J because J is an ideal and closed under addition.
      have h₁ : a ∈ J := ha.2
      have h₂ : b ∈ J := hb.2
      exact Ideal.add_mem J h₁ h₂
    exact ⟨h₁, h₂⟩

intro a b ha hb
    have haI : a ∈ I := ha.1
    have haJ : a ∈ J := ha.2
    have hbI : b ∈ I := hb.1
    have hbJ : b ∈ J := hb.2
    have hsumI : a + b ∈ I := I.add_mem haI hbI
    have hsumJ : a + b ∈ J := J.add_mem haJ hbJ
    exact ⟨hsumI, hsumJ⟩

    · exact J.add_mem ha.2 hb.2

  mul_mem_left' := by
    intro a b hab
    have haI : a ∈ I := hab.1
    have haJ : a ∈ J := hab.2
    have hI : b * a ∈ I := I.mul_mem_left b haI
    have hJ : b * a ∈ J := J.mul_mem_left b haJ
    exact ⟨hI, hJ⟩

intro a ha b hb
    -- We need to show that if a and b are in the intersection of I and J, then a + b is also in the intersection.
    have h1 : a + b ∈ I := by
      -- Since a is in I and b is in I, a + b is in I.
      have ha_I : a ∈ I := by exact ha.1
      have hb_I : b ∈ I := by exact hb.1
      exact I.add_mem ha_I hb_I
    have h2 : a + b ∈ J := by
      -- Since a is in J and b is in J, a + b is in J.
      have ha_J : a ∈ J := by exact ha.2
      have hb_J : b ∈ J := by exact hb.2
      exact J.add_mem ha_J hb_J
    -- Therefore, a + b is in the intersection of I and J.
    exact ⟨h1, h2⟩
